examples: The streets of the Old City are narrow and labyrinthine.
Those that register find the paperwork labyrinthine and confusing.
Ikea is an old hand at herding customers through a labyrinthine store layout.
Buying a house is still a labyrinthine process with time-sensitive signatures, courier visits and faxes.
In the labyrinthine world of Malaysian politics, smoke in the kitchen means there is something cooking.
